question: How many students scored at least 25%
solution: Number of students who scored atleast 25% = 5 + 3 + 8 = 16

question: Divide 1101001<sub>two</sub> by 101<sub>two</sub>
An article when sold for ₦230.00 makes a 15% profit. Find the profit or loss % if it was sold for ₦180.00
What is the general term of the sequence 3, 8, 13, 18, ...?
solution:
Given the sequence 3, 8, 13, 18, ... which is an arithmetic sequence

a = 3

d = T<sub>2</sub>  - T<sub>1</sub>  = 8 - 3 = 5

The general term of an A.P is:

T<sub>n</sub>  = a + (n - 1)d

⇒ T<sub>n</sub> = 3 + (n - 1)5

= T<sub>n</sub> = 3 + 5n - 5

∴ T<sub>n</sub> = 5n - 2

question: The locus of a point equidistant from two intersecting lines is

option.a: where the sum of the distances of two focal points is fixed
option.b: the collection of points that are equally distant from a fixed point and a line
option.c: the perpendicular bisector of the lines
option.d: pair of bisectors of the angles between the two lines

solution: The locus of a point equidistant from two intersecting lines is pair of bisectors of the angles between the two lines.

question: Calculate, correct to three significant figures, the length AB in the diagram above.
solution:
sinA/a = sinB/b = sinC/c
⟹sin82<sup>0</sup>/43.2 = sin56<sup>0</sup>/AB
⟹AB × sin82<sup>0</sup> = 43.2 × sin56<sup>0</sup>
∴ AB = (43.2 × sin56<sup>0</sup>)/sin82<sup>0</sup>  = 36.2cm (to 3 s.f)

question: The angle of elevation and depression of the top and bottom of another building, measured from the top of a 24 m tall building, is 30° and 60°, respectively. Determine the second building's height.
solution:
The height of the second building H = h + 24

tan θ = opp/adj
tan 30° = h/x
⟹3√3 = h/x
⟹x = √3 = 3h
⟹x = 3h/√3 ....(i)

tan 60° = 24/x
⟹√3 = 24x
⟹x√3 = 24
⟹x = 24/√3 ....(ii)

Equate equation (i) and (ii)

⟹3h/√3 = 24/√3
⟹ 3h = 24

⟹h = 24/3 = 8m

∴The height of the second building = 8 + 24 = 32m

question: The ages of students in a small primary school were recorded in the table below.  Estimate the mean
In a group of 500 people, 350 people can speak English, and 400 people can speak French. Find how many people can speak both languages.
solution:
Let F be the set of people who can speak French and E be the set of people who can speak English. Then,

n(F) = 400

n(E) = 350

n(F ∪ E) = 500

We have to find n(F ∩ E).

Now, n(F ∪ E) = n(F) + n(E) – n(F ∩ E)

⇒ 500 = 400 + 350 – n(F ∩ E)

⇒ n(F ∩ E) = 750 – 500 = 250.

∴ 250 people can speak both languages.

question: At simple interest, a man made a deposit of some money in the bank. The amount in his bank account after 10 years is three times the money deposited. If the interest rate stays the same, after how many years will the amount be five times the money deposited?
solution:
Amount (A) = Principal (P) + Interest (I) i.e. A = P + I

1 = PTR/100
A = 3P; T = 10 years (Given)

Since A = P + I

⟹3P=P+P×10×R/100
⟹3P=P+10PR/100
⟹3P=P+PR/10
⟹3P−P=PR/10
⟹2P=PR/10
⟹2P/1=PR/10
⟹ PR = 20P

∴ R = 20%

Since the rate stays the same

A = 5P; R = 20%;T =?; A  =P + I

⟹5P = P + (p × T × 20)/100
⟹5P − P = (2PT)/10
⟹4P = (2PT)/10
⟹2P = PT/10
⟹ PT = 20P

∴ T = 20 years

question: Find the compound interest (CI) on ₦15,700 for 2 years at 8% per annum compounded annually.
Find the value of t, if the distance between the points P(–3, –14) and Q(t, –5) is 9 units.
solution:
Let the given points be:

P(-3, -14) = (x<sub>1</sub> , y<sub>1</sub>)

Q(t, -5) = (x<sub>2</sub>, y<sub>2</sub>)

PQ = 9 units (given)

Using the distance formula,

d = √ [ (x<sub>2</sub> − x<sub>1</sub> )<sup>2</sup> + (y<sub>2</sub> − y<sub>1</sub>)<sup>2</sup>]

PQ = √ [ (t − (−3))<sup>2</sup> + (−5 + 14)<sup>2</sup>]

⟹ √ [ ( t + 3)<sup>2</sup> + 81] = 9

Squaring on both sides,

⇒ (t + 3)<sup>2</sup> + 81 = 81

⇒ (t + 3)<sup>2</sup>  = 0

⇒ t + 3 = 0

∴ t = -3

question: Two numbers are respectively 35% and 80% more than a third number. The ratio of the two numbers is
solution:
Let the third number = x
Then the first number = 100% x + 35%x = 135x/100 = 1.35x (Note: 100% x=x)

The second number = 180% x = 180x/100 = 1.80x
∴ The ratio of the first number to the second number = 1.35x:1.80x=3:4

question: Make x the subject of the formula: y = (3x−9c)/(4x+5d)

A man sells different brands of an items. 1/9
 of the items he has in his shop are from Brand A, 5/8
 of the remainder are from Brand B and the rest are from Brand C. If the total number of Brand C items in the man's shop is 81, how many more Brand B items than Brand C does the shop has?
solution:
Let the total number of items in the man's shop = y
Number of Brand A's items in the man's shop = 1/9y
Remaining items = 1 - 1/9y=8/9y
Number of Brand B's items in The man's shop = 5/8of8/9y=5/9y
Total of Brand A and Brand B's items = 1/9y+5/9y=2/3y
Number of Brand C's items in the man's shop = 1 - 2/3y=1/3y
⟹1/3y = 81 (Given)

⟹y = 81 x 3 = 243

∴ The total number of items in the man's shop = 243

∴ Number of Brand B's items in the man's shop = 5/9
 x 243 = 135

∴ The number of more Brand B items than Brand C = 135 - 81 =54

question: Calculate, correct to three significant figures, the length of the arc AB in the diagram above.[Take π=22/7]
solution:
Consider ∆XOB and using Pythagoras theorem

13<sup>2</sup> = 12<sup>2</sup> + h<sup>2</sup>
⇒ 169 = 144 + h<sup>2</sup>
⇒ 169 - 144  =h<sup>2</sup>
⇒ 25 = h<sup>2</sup>
⇒ h = √25 = 5cm

tan θ = opp/adj
⇒ tan θ = 12/5 = 2.4

⇒ θ = tan<sup>-1</sup>(2.4)

⇒ θ = 67.38<sup>o</sup>
∠AOB = 2θ = 2 x 67.38<sup>o</sup> = 134.76<sup>o</sup>
L = θ/360<sup>o</sup>×2πr
⇒ L = 134.76/360 × 2 × (22/7) × 13 =77082.72/2520
∴ L = 30.6cm (to 3 s.f)

question: A rectangular plot of land has sides with lengths of 38 m and 52 m corrected to the nearest m. Find the range of the possible values of the area of the rectangle
solution:
The sides have been given to the nearest meter, so

51.5 m ≤ length < 52.5

37.5 m ≤ width < 38.5

Minimum area = 37.5 x 51.5 = 1931.25 m<sup>2</sup>


Maximum area = 38.5 x 52.5 = 2021.25 m<sup>2</sup>


∴ The range of the area = 1931.25 m<sup>2</sup> ≤ A < 2021.25 m<sup>2</sup>

question: A coin is thrown 3 times. What is the probability that atleast one head is obtained?
solution:
Sample space = {HHH, HHT, HTH, THH, TTH, THT, HTT, TTT}

Total number of possible outcomes = 2 x 2 x 2 = 8

favourable number of outcomes = 7

∴Pr(at least one head) = 7/8

question: If −2x<sup></sup> +6x<sup>2</sup> +17x - 21 is divided by (x+1), then the remainder is
solution:
Let p(x)=−2x<sup>3</sup> + 6x<sup>2</sup> + 17x − 21


Using the remainder theorem

Let x + 1 = 0


∴ x = −1


Since, (x+1) divides p(x), then, remainder will be p(-1)

⇒ p(-1) = -2(-1)<sup>3</sup> +6(−1)<sup>2</sup>  + 17(-1) - 21

∴ p(-1) = -30

question: Find the matrix A
The interior angle of a regular polygon is five times the size of its exterior angle. Identify the polygon.
solution:
An interior angle of a regular polygon = ((2n−4)×90)/n


An exterior angle of a regular polygon = 360/n


((2n−4)×90)/n = 5 ×  360/n (Given)
= (2n-4) x 90 = 5 x 360
= 180n - 360 = 1800
= 180n = 1800 + 360
= 180n = 2160
= n = 2160/180 = 12
The polygon has 12 sides which is dodecagon

question: How many different 8 letter words are possible using the letters of the word SYLLABUS?
solution:
SYLLABUS has 8 letters, 2S's and 2L's

8!/2!2!

question: Find the value of y, if log (y + 8) + log (y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5
solution:
log (y + 8) + log (y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5

⇒ log (y + 8)(y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5 (log ab = log a + log b)

⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> -8y + 8y - 64) = log 3<sup>2</sup> + log 5<sup>2</sup>


⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 3<sup>2</sup> + log 5<sup>2</sup>


⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 9 + log 25

⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log (9 × 25)

⇒ log(y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 225

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> - 64 = 225

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> = 225 + 64

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> = 289

⇒ y = ±√289

∴ y = ±17

question:
Determine the area of the region bounded by y = 2x<sup>2</sup> + 10 and Y = 4x + 16
.
solution:
To find the point of intersection, equate the two equations

⇒ 2x<sup>2</sup> + 10 = 4x + 16

⇒ 2x<sup>2</sup>  + 10 - 4x - 16 = 0

⇒ 2x<sup>2</sup>  - 4x - 6 = 0

Factorize

⇒ 2(x + 1)(x - 3) = 0

∴ x = -1 or 3

The curves will intersect at -1 and 3


Area = ∫<sup>b</sup> <sub>a</sub>  [upper function] - [lower function] dx

 

= ∫<sup>3</sup> <sub>1</sub> 4x + 16 − (2x<sup>2</sup>+10)dx
= ∫<sup>3</sup> <sub>1</sub> − 2x<sup>2</sup> + 4x + 6dx
= ((−2x<sup>3</sup>)/3 + (2x<sup>2</sup>) + 6x)<sup>3</sup> <sub>1</sub>
= ((−2(3)<sup>3</sup>)/3 + 2(3)<sup>2</sup> + 6(3)) − ((−2(−1)<sup>3</sup>)/3 + 2(−1)<sup>2</sup> + 6(−1))
= 18 -  (10/3)
= 18 + 10/3
= 64/3

question: Let '*' and '^' be two binary operations such that a * b = a<sup>2</sup>b and a ^ b = 2a + b. Find (-4 * 2) ^ (7 * -1).
Solve for x: 3(x – 1) ≤ 2 (x – 3)
solution:
3(x – 1) ≤ 2 (x – 3)

= 3x – 3 ≤ 2x – 6

Add 3 to both sides

= 3x – 3+ 3 ≤ 2x – 6+ 3

= 3x ≤ 2x – 3

Subtract 2x from both the sides

= 3x – 2x ≤ 2x – 3 – 2x

∴ x ≤ -3

question: Calculate the mean deviation of the first five prime numbers.

The area A of a circle is increasing at a constant rate of 1.5 cm <sup>2</sup>s<sup>−1</sup>
. Find, to 3 significant figures, the rate at which the radius r of the circle is increasing when the area of the circle is 2 cm<sup>2</sup>
.
solution:
Area of a circle (A) = πr<sup>2</sup>


Given

dA/dt=1.5 cm<sup>2</sup>s<sup>-1</sup>


dr/dt = ?

A = 2cm<sup>2</sup>


Now

2 = πr <sup>2</sup> 


= r<sup>2</sup> = 2/π


r = √(2/π) cm = 0.798cm

dr/dt = dA/dt × dr/dt


dA/dr= 2πr (differentiating A = πr<sup>2</sup>)


dr/dA=1/2πr


dr/dt=1.5 × 1/(2 × π × 0.798) = 1.5 × 0.199


dr/dt = 0.299cms<sup>−1</sup> (to 3 s.f)

question: Solve the following quadratic inequality: x<sup>2</sup> − x - 4 ≤ 2
solution:
x<sup>2</sup> − x − 4 ≤ 2

Subtract two from both sides to rewrite it in the quadratic standard form:
= x<sup>2</sup> − x −4 −2 ≤ 2 −2

= x<sup>2</sup> − x − 6 ≤ 0

Now set it = 0 and factor and solve like normal.
= x<sup>2</sup> − x - 6 =0
= (x − 3)(x + 2) = 0
x + 2 = 0 or x - 3 = 0
x = -2 or x = 3
So the two zeros are -2 and 3, and will mark the boundaries of our answer interval. To find out if the interval is between -2 and 3, or on either side, we simply take a test point between -2 and 3 (for instance, x = 0) and evaluate the original inequality.
= x<sup>2</sup> − x − 4 ≤ 2

= (0)<sup>2</sup> − (0) − 4 ≤ 2

= 0 − 0 − 4 ≤ 2

−4 ≤ 2

Since the above is a true statement, we know that the solution interval is between -2 and 3, the same region where we picked our test point. Since the original inequality was less than or equal, we include the endpoints.
∴ −2 ≤ x ≤ 3.

question: A bag contains 8 red balls and some white balls. If the probability of drawing a white ball is half of the probability of drawing a red ball then find the probability of drawing a red ball and a white ball if the balls are drawn without replacement.
solution:
Let the number of white balls be n

The number of red balls = 8

Now, the probability of drawing a white ball = n/n+8
The probability of drawing a red ball = 8/n+8
Since Pr(White ball) = 1/2
 x Pr(Red ball)

∴n/(n + 8)= 1/2 × 8/(n + 8)
= n/(n + 8) = 4/(n + 8)
∴n = 4
Number of white balls = 4

The possible number of outcomes = n + 8 = 4 + 8 = 12

Pr(Red ball and White ball) = Pr(Red ball) x Pr(White ball)

Pr(Red ball) = 8/12
Pr(Red ball) = 4/11
Pr(Red ball and white ball) = 8/12 × 4/11
∴ Pr(Red ball and white ball) = 8/33

question: Factorize: 16x<sup>4</sup>−y<sup>4</sup>
solution:
16x<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= 2<sup>4</sup>x<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= (2x)<sup>4</sup> − y<sup>4</sup>


= ((2x)<sup>2</sup>)<sup>2</sup> − (y<sup>2</sup>)<sup>2</sup>


Using a<sup>2</sup> − b<sup>2</sup> = (a - b)(a + b) identity

= ((2x)<sup>2</sup>−y<sup>2</sup>)((2x)<sup>2</sup>+y<sup>2</sup>)


Using the identity one more time

= (2x − y)(2x + y)((2x)<sup>2</sup> + y<sup>2</sup>)


∴ (2x − y)(2x + y)(4x<sup>2</sup> + y<sup>2</sup>)

question: A committee of 5 people is to be chosen from a group of 6 men and 4 women. How many committees are possible if there is to be a majority of women?
solution:
If the majority are women implies that:

3 Women and 2 Men Or 4 Women and 1 Man

= <sup>4</sup>C<sub>3</sub> × <sup>6</sup>C<sub>2</sub> + <sup>4</sup>C<sub>4</sub> × <sup>6</sup>C<sub>1</sub>
= 4 × 15 +1 × 6
= 60 + 6 = 66

question:
The diagram above is a circle with centre C. P, Q and S are points on the circumference. PS and SR are tangents to the circle. ∠PSR = 36<sup>o</sup>
. Find ∠PQR
solution:
From ∆PSR

|PS| = |SR| (If two tangents are drawn from an external point of the circle, then they are of equal lengths)

∴ ∆PSR is isosceles

∠PSR + ∠SRP + ∠SPR = 180
 <sup>o</sup> (sum of angles in a triangle)

Since |PS| = |SR|; ∠SRP = ∠SPR

⇒ ∠PSR + ∠SRP + ∠SRP = 180<sup>o</sup>


∠PSR + 2∠SRP = 180<sup>o</sup>


36<sup>o</sup>
 + 2∠SRP = 180<sup>o</sup>


2∠SRP = 180<sup>o</sup>
 - 36<sup>o</sup>


2∠SRP = 144<sup>o</sup>
∠SRP = 144<sup>o</sup>/2=72<sup>o</sup>
∠SRP = ∠PQR (angle formed by a tangent and chord is equal to the angle in the alternate segment)

∴ ∠PQR = 72<sup>o</sup>

question: If (3−√3)/(2+√3) = a + b√3, what are the values a and b?
Evaluate the following limit: lim <sub>(x→2)</sub> (x<sup>2</sup> + 4x − 12)/(x<sup>2</sup> − 2x)
Find the value of y if 402<sub>y</sub> =102<sub>ten</sub>
Find the area, to the nearest cm<sup>2</sup>, of the triangle whose sides are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4 and whose perimeter is 180 cm.
solution:
Let the length of the sides of triangle be 2x, 3x and 4x.

Perimeter of triangle = 180cm

⇒ 2x + 3x + 4x = 180
⇒ 9x = 180
⇒ x=180/9
 = 20 cm

Then the sides of the triangle are:

2x = 2 × 20 = 40 cm; 3x = 3 × 20 = 60cm and 4x = 4 × 20 = 80cm

Using Heron's formula

Area of triangle = s√(s−a)(s−b)(s−c)
Where s = (a + b + c)/2
Let a = 40cm, b = 60cm, c = 80cm and s = (40 + 60 + 80)/2 =180/2 = 90cm

⇒ A = √90(90−40)(90−60)(90−80)=√90×50×30×10=√(1350000)
∴ A =1162cm<sup>2</sup> (to the nearest cm<sup>2</sup>)

question: If a car runs at a constant speed and takes 4.5 hrs to run a distance of 225 km, what time will it take to run 150 km?
solution:
Let T be the time taken and D is the distance

For the case given in the problem, the distance covered is directly proportional to the time taken i.e D α T

⇒ D = ST where S is the constant speed

D = 225 km and T = 4.5 hrs

∴ 225 = S x 4.5

S = 225/4.5 = 50 km/hr

When D = 150 km

D = ST

150 = 50 × T

T = 150/50 = 3 hrs

So it will take 3 hrs

question: Evaluate ∫<sup>1</sup> <sub>0</sub> 4x−6<sup>3</sup>√x<sup>2</sup>−dx
A boat sails 8 km north from P to Q and then sails 6 km west from Q to R. Calculate the bearing of R from P. Give your answer to the nearest degree.
solution:
tan θ = opp/adj = RQ/QP = 6/8
tan θ = 0.75

θ = tan<sup>−1</sup>(0.75) = 36.87°


∴ The bearing of R from P = 360° - 36.87° = 323°
 (to the nearest degree)
